Safaricom services have been experiencing instability and slowness today. While some payments are going through successfully, callbacks are not being received consistently to confirm payments. In some cases, the STK Push does not prompt customers to enter their PIN at all. Response times are also unusually slow, further affecting transaction completion.

The error response below  from safaricom indicates a temporary service outage on Safaricom’s side, and retries may be required until the service stabilizes:

Error Code: 500.002.1001
Message: Service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.

We are monitoring the situation closely and will provide updates once the service becomes stable.

For direct enquiry on the issue reach out directly to safaricom here apisupport@safaricom.co.ke

The International Criminal Court ruled on Thursday that the trial of Rodrigo Duterte, the former president of the Philippines, on charges of crimes against humanity could proceed. The case would be a milestone for the country, in which there has been little legal reckoning for Duterte’s deadly crackdown against drug dealers and users. https://trib.al/V1d5Bqg

The world’s largest condom maker is raising prices of its products by up to 30%, warning that the Iran war could disrupt production. The Malaysian condom company, Karex, which produces about five billion condoms a year, blamed a surge in raw material prices, global shipping disruptions and higher freight costs for the price increases.

Read more: https://trib.al/zqfToN7

Next on President Trump’s renovation tour of Washington, D.C.: the Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool, which he called “filthy” and “dirty” and in need of a major upgrade. The reflecting pool is more than 2,000 feet long and has been the site of many historic events, including Martin Luther King Jr.’s “I Have a Dream” speech. Trump said he would be using a contractor he knows from his years in real estate.

Read more: https://trib.al/g7LdZAu

President Trump provoked a broad backlash when he posted a transcript from a right-wing podcast in which the host referred to China and India as “hellhole” places and said recent immigrants from those countries had not “integrated” into America as “European Americans” had.

President Trump announced a three-week extension of a cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon that had been set to expire in a few days, after hosting a meeting between Israeli and Lebanese diplomats at the White House on Thursday.

Read more: https://trib.al/j4TFnfi

Republicans and Democrats in Congress condemned a plan President Trump is weighing to send Afghans who aided the American military campaign against the Taliban to the Democratic Republic of Congo. Lawmakers in both parties said they instead wanted to see Trump restore a program he blocked that allows eligible Afghans who have undergone security vetting to settle in the U.S. and have a pathway to permanent residency.

“For those individuals that helped our young men and women, once they’ve been vetted thoroughly, I have no objections to them being in the United States,” Senator Mike Rounds of South Dakota said.

Read more: https://trib.al/WLXAudK
